key_findings.title: FDA Black Box Warning Drugs
key_findings.finding: BBW drugs 59.4%F vs non-BBW 52.6%F (p=5.6e-57). TZDs 91.7%F drive excess.
key_findings.significance: Safety-critical drugs have MORE female-biased AE profiles

key_findings.title: Signal Entropy
key_findings.finding: Distance from 50% perfectly predicts entropy (ρ=-1.000). Low-entropy drugs skew male (31.2%F).
key_findings.significance: Drugs with consistent sex direction exist and tend male-biased

key_findings.title: Gene-Bridged Pathway Atlas
key_findings.finding: 94.7% bridge overlap. 328 pathways. STAT3/ALK signaling 81.4%F. Calcitonin receptors 13.5%F.
key_findings.significance: First Reactome pathway-level sex bias atlas via drug targets

key_findings.title: PPI Hub Targeting (Bridged)
key_findings.finding: Hub drugs +3.4pp more female (51.9% vs 48.5%). PPI degree→%F: ρ=0.118 (p=0.01).
key_findings.significance: Drugs targeting network hubs show mild female bias

key_findings.title: Drug-AE Specificity (Gini)
key_findings.finding: Gini concentration does NOT predict sex bias (ρ=-0.016). Breadth does (ρ=0.200).
key_findings.significance: More AEs = more female-biased, but concentration irrelevant

key_findings.title: Drug Class Interaction
key_findings.finding: CPI×Biologic most discordant (ρ=-0.110). NSAID×Biologic most concordant (ρ=0.397).
key_findings.significance: Drug classes show consistent within-class AE sex patterns

key_findings.title: Named Reactome Pathway Atlas
key_findings.finding: DNA Processes 67.0%F most female category. Cytokines 30.5%F most male.
key_findings.significance: Pathway categories reveal biological sex bias patterns

key_findings.title: Signal Stability
key_findings.finding: Low-High confidence ρ=0.646 (p=4.4e-40). Female bias increases with confidence: 53.8%→67.0%F.
key_findings.significance: Sex differential signals are STABLE and get STRONGER with more data
